PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: SpeakUp deep talks
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: a conversation that went below the surface; write what the pair reached for and what the exchange exposed, using only the supplied lines and context

instructions.li: deep talk - let the meaning sit between the pair: why this subject mattered today and what changed by saying it aloud
deep talk - use a supplied line as evidence, never as permission to invent the rest of the conversation
deep talk - a dream or psychic vision is intimate evidence, not proof of a new event beyond what the context states

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: thoughtful, intimate, and a little exposed

tones.li: quietly searching, with the weight below ordinary words
close and reflective, two people testing what can be said

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: SpeakUp jokes
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: the day's running comedy; remember who kept trying to be funny, who laughed, and who groaned without inventing a punchline

instructions.li: jokes - use the supplied lines to show the pair's rhythm, not to manufacture a better joke
jokes - notice whether the humor brought relief, affection, embarrassment, or irritation
jokes - keep it light unless the pawn's mood or relationship makes the reaction carry more

tones.li: fond and quick, laughter breaking up the work

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: SpeakUp prisoner talks
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: talking through the bars; write the rapport, wariness, refusal, or strange intimacy of guarding someone, from the eligible colonist's view when the prisoner cannot own a diary

instructions.li: prisoner talk - keep the imbalance of freedom and control present without inventing cruelty or trust
prisoner talk - use acceptance or refusal as the emotional turn, not as a mechanical recruitment report
prisoner talk - a repeated skill conversation can become familiar without pretending the bars disappeared

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: wary and close, shaped by the barrier between them

tones.li: guarded, with rapport arriving by inches
uneasy and observant, familiarity under confinement

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: SpeakUp thought reactions
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: passing reactions to what people were already carrying; use them as the day's social texture, not as separate dramatic events

instructions.li: thought reaction - choose only the supplied reactions that reveal a pattern in the pawn's day
thought reaction - show how feelings leaked into ordinary talk without inventing their cause
thought reaction - keep frequent remarks folded small; texture matters more than a transcript

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.tone: brief, observant, and woven into the day

tones.li: fragmentary and honest, feelings escaping in passing
low-key and cumulative, small reactions forming a pattern

PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.label: SpeakUp chatter
PawnDiary.DiaryInteractionGroupDef.instruction: ordinary SpeakUp conversation - treat it as chitchat unless the pair's mood, need, or relationship tension makes the small moment land harder

instructions.li: SpeakUp chatter - use the supplied line if present; otherwise write low-stakes back-and-forth without inventing a quote
SpeakUp chatter - focus on the feeling under the small talk: ease, awkwardness, pressure, or distance
SpeakUp chatter - let the ordinary rhythm matter only if the pawn's state makes it matter

tones.li: loose and conversational, like ordinary talk passing through the room
plain and observant, letting tiny social pressure carry the weight
